name: space_debris_removal_entangled_foreign_solar_panel
premise: Orbital safety lead Casey Keane must authorize a net-capture debris removal that may entangle a defunct but still-owned foreign research solar panel registered to an allied agency. Milo Young models a ninety-two percent success chance that clears a corridor for crewed station resupply. Failure could be interpreted as hostile debris tampering under tense treaty talks. Waiting forces the crew mission onto a higher-debris path with documented conjunction risk.

agents.name: Casey Keane
agents.identity: I am the orbital safety lead responsible for conjunction management, treaty-sensitive operations, and crew mission risk budgets.
agents.goal: I want debris cleared without triggering diplomatic incidents over allied hardware entanglement.

agents.name: Milo Young
agents.identity: I am an orbital dynamics specialist modeling capture torques and tether snap scenarios for net-based removal.
agents.goal: I want the capture scrubbed if ownership ambiguity remains because entangling allied panels could poison cooperation on bigger debris threats.

choices: I will scrub this capture window and reroute the crew mission along the higher-debris path with extra shielding, accepting elevated micrometeoroid risk metrics.
I will proceed with capture after securing written allied waiver within six hours and attach a tether breakaway fuse, accepting diplomatic delay risk to the launch window.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Launch window closes in six hours and mission control is polling go-no-go. Casey Keane must choose between scrubbing capture or proceeding with a waivered capture. What does Casey Keane do?
